description: In 2012, the OUR Walmart campaign coordinated coordinated strikes and protests on Black Friday to demand better wages and working conditions. While it didn't result in a traditional union contract, it marked a turning point in the public discourse regarding retail wages in the US.

key_events.description: National media coverage of Black Friday walkouts.
Wal-Mart releases public statement threatening legal action.
Community organizations join the protest effort.
Public sentiment stabilizes with a majority favoring the 'living wage' demands.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring support for non-unionized movements and how they gain legitimacy in public opinion over time.
